senses.definition: a technology that converts written text into spoken voice output

examples.x: The text to speech feature on my phone allows me to listen to articles and messages while I'm driving.
The software uses advanced algorithms for text to speech conversion, producing natural-sounding speech.
The accessibility options on the computer include a text to speech function for users with visual impairments.
The website offers a text to speech option for users who prefer to listen rather than read.
Text to speech technology has improved significantly in recent years, making it easier for people with disabilities to access digital content.

senses.definition: a technology that allows devices to be connected to a computer system and used without requiring extensive installation or configuration

examples.x: The new printer is plug and play, so you can set it up in minutes without needing any additional software.
The gaming console features plug and play functionality, allowing you to connect it to your TV and start playing immediately.
The USB microphone is plug and play, making it easy to use with your computer for recording audio.
The smart home devices are plug and play, allowing you to easily add them to your network and control them with a smartphone app.
The external hard drive is plug and play, so you can quickly transfer files between computers without any hassle.

senses.definition: a small hardware device that connects to a computer, smartphone, or other electronic device to provide additional functionality

examples.x: A Bluetooth dongle allows users to connect wireless peripherals to their computers.
A TV dongle can be used to stream content from a smartphone or computer to a television screen.
Wireless mouse and keyboard sets often come with a dongle for easy connection to a computer.
Some software licensing systems utilize dongles to authorize and authenticate the use of the software on a particular device.

senses.definition: a small, handheld computer that fits in the palm of one's hand
the process of converting data into a coded form to prevent unauthorized access

examples.x: The email was sent with end-to-end encryption to safeguard its contents during transmission.
Many messaging apps use encryption to protect users' conversations from eavesdropping.
Online transactions often employ encryption to secure financial information and prevent unauthorized access.
Virtual private networks (VPNs) use encryption to secure internet connections and protect user privacy.
Secure websites use encryption (HTTPS) to encrypt data transmitted between the user's browser and the server.

senses.definition: a set of rules used by digital systems to decide what content to show users based on their behavior and preferences

examples.x: The search engine uses complex algorithms to rank web pages based on relevance and authority.
Social media platforms use algorithms to personalize users' news feeds, showing them content based on their interests and behavior.
Online retailers use recommendation algorithms to suggest products to customers based on their browsing and purchasing history.
Streaming services use algorithms to predict user preferences and recommend movies and TV shows they may enjoy.
News websites use algorithms to curate and prioritize articles for readers based on factors such as timeliness and popularity.

senses.definition: a type of switch used in user interfaces to turn a single setting on or off

examples.x: The app's toggle button swiftly switches between light and dark modes.
Navigating through options is a breeze with the website's toggle button.
The email client's toggle button filters messages by unread status.
Messaging app's toggle button swiftly switches between inbox and sent items.
Enable or disable automatic updates with a simple toggle button.

senses.definition: a program that automatically loads and initializes the operating system on a computer

examples.x: Bootstrap is a popular front-end framework for developing responsive and mobile-first websites.
The bootstrap process involves loading a minimal set of files and resources to start up a computer system.
Many startups use bootstrap financing methods such as crowdfunding or bootstrapping to fund their initial development and growth.
The term "bootstrap" is also used in the context of bootstrapping algorithms, which use a simple initial estimate to iteratively improve a solution.
In computing, "bootstrap" can refer to the process of loading and initializing a computer's operating system.

senses.definition: the time it takes for data to travel from one point to another in a network

examples.x: Slow internet can result in increased latency.
High latency causes delays in online gaming.
Gamers prefer networks with minimal latency.

senses.definition: the ability of different systems, software, or devices to seamlessly communicate, exchange data, and work together effectively

examples.x: The new software update improves interoperability between devices.
Interoperability enables seamless data transfer between apps.
Our goal is to achieve full interoperability across all platforms.
Engineers are working to enhance interoperability between networks.
Interoperability is crucial for the success of interconnected technologies.

senses.definition: a set of protocols and tools that allows different software applications to communicate with each other
the maximum rate of data transfer of an electronic communication system
a network for communication between computers, usually within a building

examples.x: A local area network (LAN) is a network that connects computers and devices within a limited geographical area, such as a home, office, or school.
Home networks, consisting of multiple devices connected to a router, are a common example of a local area network used for internet access and file sharing within a household.

senses.definition: a computer or device on a network that provides services, resources, or data to other computers or devices, known as clients

examples.x: The web server acts as a host, serving website content to users who access it.
In a peer-to-peer network, each computer can function as both a host and a client.
A file server on a network serves as a host, storing and sharing files with connected devices.
Printers connected to a network can be hosts, allowing multiple users to print documents.
Cloud hosting services provide virtual hosts where businesses can store and access data.

senses.definition: a type of educational software or online materials designed for teaching specific subjects or skills

examples.x: Courseware refers to educational materials, software, or online platforms used for teaching and learning in academic institutions.
Many universities provide courseware to students, including lecture slides, textbooks, and interactive tutorials, to supplement classroom instruction.
Online learning platforms, such as Coursera and edX, offer a wide range of courseware covering various subjects and disciplines.
Courseware can include multimedia content, quizzes, assignments, and discussion forums to engage learners and facilitate understanding.
Teachers and instructors often customize courseware to meet the specific needs of their students, tailoring content and activities to enhance learning outcomes.

senses.definition: a harmful program that pretends to be legitimate and can give unauthorized access to a device

examples.x: The trojan horse disguised itself as a harmless email attachment.
A trojan horse can steal personal information without being detected.
His computer was infected by a trojan horse that appeared as an update.
She unknowingly downloaded a trojan horse that compromised her data.
Many trojan horse infections occur through fake downloads or links.

senses.definition: to set up a system, device, software, or components in a specific way to achieve a desired functionality

examples.x: Before using the new printer, you need to configure it by installing the necessary drivers and adjusting settings.
Network administrators must configure routers to ensure secure and efficient data transmission.
Users need to configure email clients with server details to send and receive emails.
To enhance security, individuals should configure privacy settings on social media accounts.
IT professionals configure firewalls to regulate network traffic and protect against unauthorized access.

senses.definition: to send a signal to a computer and wait for a reply as a way to test the connection

examples.x: You can ping a website to check if it is reachable and measure the round-trip time (RTT) for data packets to travel to and from the server.
I will ping the server to see if it's responding to requests.
Please ping the network printer to check if it's online.
If you suspect a network problem, you can ping the router to verify the connection.
To troubleshoot internet connectivity, you can ping a website like google.com to check if your computer can reach it.

senses.definition: a technology that overlays computer-generated information, such as images or data, onto the real world, enhancing the user's perception and interaction with their environment

examples.x: Augmented reality (AR) technology overlays digital information, such as images, videos, or 3D models, onto the real world through a device like a smartphone or AR glasses.
Popular AR apps like Pokémon GO use augmented reality to superimpose virtual creatures onto real-world environments, allowing players to interact with them.
Retailers use augmented reality to provide customers with immersive shopping experiences, such as trying on virtual clothing or visualizing furniture in their homes before making a purchase.
Augmented reality is also used in education, allowing students to explore virtual anatomy models or historical landmarks in a more engaging way.
Industrial applications of augmented reality include training simulations, remote assistance, and visualizing complex machinery or architectural designs in real-world contexts.

senses.definition: to make a process, task, or system operate automatically, often through the use of technology or machinery, reducing the need for manual intervention

examples.x: In manufacturing, companies automate assembly lines to increase production speed and accuracy.
Businesses often automate payroll processes to efficiently manage employee compensation.
Homeowners use smart home devices to automate tasks such as controlling lights, thermostats, and security systems.
Data backup processes are often automated to ensure regular and secure backups of important information.
Software developers use continuous integration tools to automate the testing and deployment of code.

senses.definition: a part of a computer responsible for storing and controlling data where users cannot easily access

examples.x: The back end of a website comprises the server, database, and application logic that work together to process and store data submitted by users.
Database administrators manage the back end by optimizing database performance, implementing data backups, and monitoring system health.
When users interact with a web application, their actions trigger requests to the back end, which processes the data and generates responses visible on the front end.

senses.definition: a part of a computer that is easily accessible and one uses constantly

examples.x: The front end of a computer typically refers to the user interface, including the monitor, keyboard, mouse, and other input devices.
In software development, the front end of a web application comprises the user-facing components, such as the layout, design, and interactive elements visible in a web browser.
When using a computer, the front end is the part that users interact with directly, such as clicking icons, typing text, or navigating menus to access applications and perform tasks.

senses.definition: an individual who engages in unauthorized attempts to access computer systems, networks, or software, often with malicious intent

examples.x: Security experts work tirelessly to defend computer systems against crackers attempting unauthorized access.
The company's cybersecurity team detected a cracker's attempt to breach their network and promptly took preventive measures.
The cracker gained access to the database and stole sensitive customer information, leading to a data breach.
The cracker used sophisticated techniques to bypass the firewall and gain unauthorized control over the server.
Educational programs on cybersecurity emphasize the importance of ethical behavior and discourage individuals from becoming crackers.
